content:
# Hebrews Chapter 10 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/HEB_009_page_002.jpg, HEB_010_page_002.jpg

1. For the law, having a shadow of the good things to come, not the very image of the things, can never, by the same sacrifices which they offer continually year by year, make perfect those who draw near.

2. Otherwise, would they not have ceased to be offered, because the worshipers, having once been cleansed, would no longer have consciousness of sins?

3. But in them [there is] a reminder of sins year by year.

4. For [it is] impossible [for the] blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.

5. Therefore, coming into the world, he says, "Sacrifice and offering you did not desire, but a body you prepared for me;

6. in whole burnt offerings and [offerings] for sin you took no pleasure.

7. Then I said, 'Behold, I have come — in the scroll of the book it is written of me — to do your will, O God.'"

8. Saying above, "Sacrifices and offerings and whole burnt offerings and [offerings] for sin you did not desire, nor took pleasure [in them]" — which are offered according to the law —

9. then he said, "Behold, I have come to do your will." He takes away the first that he may establish the second.

10. By which will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Yeshua Christos once for all.

11. And every priest stands daily ministering and offering the same sacrifices many times, which are never able to take away sins;

12. but this [one], having offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, sat down at [the] right [hand] of God,

13. from then on waiting until his enemies are made a footstool of his feet.

14. For by one offering he has perfected for all time those who are being sanctified.

15. And the Holy Spirit also testifies to us, for after having said,

16. "This [is] the covenant which I will make with them after those days, says [the] Lord: putting my laws upon their hearts, and upon their mind I will write them,"

17. [he adds,] "their sins and their lawless deeds I will remember no more."

18. Now where there is forgiveness of these, [there is] no longer an offering for sin.

19. Having therefore, brothers, boldness for the entrance into the holy places by the blood of Yeshua,

20. by a new and living way which he inaugurated for us through the veil, that is, his flesh,

21. and a great priest over the house of God,

22. let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, having [our] hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience and [our] body washed with clean water.

23. Let us hold fast the confession of the hope without wavering, for the [one] who promised [is] faithful.

24. And let us consider one another for [the] stirring up of love and good works,

25. not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together, as [is the] habit of some, but encouraging, and so much the more as you see the day drawing near.

26. For [if] we sin willfully after receiving the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains a sacrifice for sins,

27. but a certain fearful expectation of judgment and a fury of fire about to consume the adversaries.

28. Anyone who set aside the law of Moses dies without mercy on [the testimony of] two or three witnesses.

29. How much worse punishment, do you think, will be deserved by the [one] who trampled the Son of God and regarded as common the blood of the covenant by which he was sanctified and insulted the Spirit of grace?

30. For we know the [one] who said, "Vengeance [is] mine, I will repay." And again, "The Lord will judge his people."

31. [It is] a fearful [thing] to fall into [the] hands of [the] living God.

32. But remember the former days in which, having been enlightened, you endured a great struggle with sufferings,

33. on the one hand being publicly exposed to reproaches and afflictions, and on the other becoming partners with those so treated.

34. For you had compassion on the prisoners and accepted with joy the seizure of your possessions, knowing that you yourselves have a better and lasting possession.

35. Therefore do not throw away your boldness, which has a great reward.

36. For you have need of endurance, so that having done the will of God, you may receive the promise.

37. For "yet a very little while, the coming [one] will come and will not delay.

38. But my righteous one will live by faith, and if he shrinks back, my soul has no pleasure in him."

39. But we are not of shrinking back unto destruction, but of faith unto [the] preservation of [the] soul.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.5 — "A body you prepared for me":** sōma de katērtisō moi — The LXX of Psalm 40:6 reads "a body you prepared for me" where the Hebrew reads "ears you have dug for me." The author follows the LXX, and the change is theologically significant: the body prepared for Christ is the body offered in sacrifice.

2. **v.20 — "Through the veil, that is, his flesh":** The veil of the temple = Christ's flesh. When the veil was torn (Mark 15:38), access to God was opened. Christ's flesh was the barrier that, when broken, opened the way.

3. **v.29 — "Trampled the Son of God":** Three charges against the apostate: (1) trampled the Son of God, (2) profaned the covenant blood, (3) insulted the Spirit of grace. A Trinitarian structure of offense.

— EMET, AI Translator
